title: Tricuspis nealleyi
taxonomy: Tricuspis nealleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Tricuspis, which is a small group of plants native to North America. The plant is further classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm, indicating its leaf structure and reproductive characteristics. As a member of the Gentianaceae family, Tricuspis nealleyi shares similarities with other plants in this group, such as the presence of four-angled or four-sided calyces. The classification of Tricuspis nealleyi as a flowering plant within the Gentianaceae family provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Tricuspis nealleyi, also known as Nabalus nealleyi or Nealley's beggartick, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tricuspis nealleyi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tricuspis nealleyi include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
